title: Add native BLE UART multi-notify SLIP chunking coverage
description: Final review of project-LICHEN-t2hn.22.2 found that the new ztest fake GATT notify backend captures only the most recent notification and the connected send test forces a single notification with MTU 64. Add native_sim coverage that exercises ble_uart_send_slip() with an owner-held connection and a small MTU so multiple notifications are emitted, then assert chunk count, chunk ordering, and full SLIP payload reconstruction.
acceptance_criteria: ble_app_owner or a dedicated native BLE UART native_sim test proves ble_uart_send_slip() emits ordered multi-notify chunks through the owner-managed connection for an escaped SLIP payload; ref/unref counts remain balanced on success and injected notify failure.
notes:
2026-07-02 no-hardware implementation: extended native BLE UART test coverage so the ztest notify backend records per-notification chunk lengths, then exercised ble_uart_send_slip() through the owner-managed native connection with MTU 8. The escaped SLIP payload reconstructs to 15 bytes emitted as three ordered 5-byte notifications; injected notify failure stops after the first 5-byte chunk and keeps connection ref/unref counts balanced.

Code review: Ramanujan review clean; Herschel review clean; Zeno review clean. Reviewers checked chunk ordering/reconstruction, reset/bounds behavior for chunk_len[8], success/failure ref/unref balance, and native_sim scenario selection. No P0/P1/P2 findings.

Local verification: git diff --check passed.

EC2 Zephyr validation: builder i-06b729ab912729bc1 in us-east-2a with EBS vol-0875d0df8bdf82a58 mounted at /mnt/lichen-zephyr.
